tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","language":"C#","stargazers_count":5977,"forks_count":5138,"open_issues_count":1379,"updated_at":"2026-04-23T15:14:40Z","created_at":"2011-12-06T23:32:47Z","topics":["azure","azure-sdk","c-sharp","dotnet","hacktoberfest","microsoft"],"private":false,"fork":false,"archived":false,"default_branch":"main"}]}

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` **Service directory:** `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58590) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed The push incremented the version for `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` from `1.0.0-beta.2` to `1.0.0-beta.3`, following the `1.0.0-beta.2` release (2026-04-23) which upgraded the API version to `2025-09-01-preview`. The package covers VM lifecycle management on Azure Stack HCI clusters, including virtual machine instances, virtual hard disks, gallery images, logical networks, storage containers, network interfaces, and security groups. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` Examples section contains only a generic link to `.NET Management Library Code Samples` — no package-specific code snippets whatsoever - The test directory `tests/Samples/` contains 21 auto-generated sample files (VM instance CRUD, VHD management, gallery image management, logical network management, storage container management, network interface management, security group management), none of which are surfaced in the README - No `samples/Readme.cs` file exists to back README snippet placeholders (unlike the sibling `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` package which has `samples/Readme.cs`) - The `Key concepts` section is a stub linking only to generic SDK docs — no description of HCI VM-specific resource model (VmInstance, LogicalNetwork, VirtualHardDisk, etc.) - The package description in the csproj (`Azure Resource Manager client SDK for Azure resource provider Hci.Vm`) is generic and doesn't describe the service
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`** — Edit Replace the `## Examples` section with the following (the snippet bodies will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after Step 2): ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm` lets you manage virtual machine workloads running on Azure Stack HCI clusters. The key resource types are: - **HciVmInstance** – A virtual machine instance running on a HCI node (child resource of an Arc machine). - **HciVmVirtualHardDisk** – A managed virtual hard disk stored on a HCI storage path. - **HciVmLogicalNetwork** – A logical (VLAN/overlay) network on a HCI cluster. - **HciVmStorageContainer** – A storage container (SMB share or local path) for placing VM disks and images. - **HciVmGalleryImage** / **HciVmMarketplaceGalleryImage** – VM OS images sourced from a local gallery or Azure Marketplace. - **HciVmNetworkInterface** – A virtual NIC attached to a logical network. ## Examples ### Authenticate the client ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_AuthClient ``` ### Create a virtual machine instance ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateVmInstance ``` ### Get a virtual machine instance ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance ``` ### List all virtual machine instances under a host machine ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_ListVmInstances ``` ### Delete a virtual machine instance ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance ``` ### Create a virtual hard disk ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateVhd ``` ### Create a logical network ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_CreateLogicalNetwork ``` ``` **File: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs`** — Create Create this file to back the README snippet placeholders above. Use the patterns from `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/samples/Readme.cs` and the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/` as a reference. A minimal starting point: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm; using Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm.Samples { public class Readme { [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task AuthClient() { #region HciVm_Readme_AuthClient TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task GetVmInstance() { #region HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); // The resource URI is the Arc machine resource ID that hosts the VM string resourceUri = "subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}"; ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri); HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId); HciVmInstanceResource result = await hciVmInstance.GetAsync(); HciVmInstanceData resourceData = result.Data; Console.WriteLine($"VM Instance id: {resourceData.Id}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task DeleteVmInstance() { #region HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string resourceUri = "subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines/{machineName}"; ResourceIdentifier hciVmInstanceResourceId = HciVmInstanceResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(resourceUri); HciVmInstanceResource hciVmInstance = client.GetHciVmInstanceResource(hciVmInstanceResourceId); await hciVmInstance.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); Console.WriteLine("VM instance deleted."); #endregion } } } ``` > **Note:** The `CreateVmInstance`, `ListVmInstances`, `CreateVhd`, and `CreateLogicalNetwork` snippets should be authored similarly, drawing from the existing generated samples in `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmInstanceResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmVirtualHardDiskCollection.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_HciVmLogicalNetworkCollection.cs`. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. Write the sample code in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/samples/Readme.cs` (created in Step 1) with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers as shown above. 2. Add snippet placeholders in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md`: ```` ```C# Snippet:HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the empty placeholder bodies with the actual code. 3. Snippet names must match exactly between the test file region and the README placeholder: - `HciVm_Readme_AuthClient` - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVmInstance` - `HciVm_Readme_GetVmInstance` - `HciVm_Readme_ListVmInstances` - `HciVm_Readme_DeleteVmInstance` - `HciVm_Readme_CreateVhd` - `HciVm_Readme_CreateLogicalNetwork` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/README.md` is mostly structured correctly but needs these updates: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | No change | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Stub | Replace with HCI VM resource model description (see Step 1) | | Examples | ❌ Empty | Add snippet placeholders (see Steps 1 & 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 azurestackhci` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from sample files and injects code into README.md placeholder blocks; verify with `git diff` that README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 azurestackhci` — only needed if public API surface changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm/` — ensures consistent formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [AzureStackHCI] Docs: Add code examples to Azure.ResourceManager.Hci.Vm README
